rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim ('improves on prior work') is underspecified; 'stronger' plausibly requires sharpening the gap statement and making the thesis load-bearing rather than descriptive.
rationale.R1.3: Performance deltas are marginal (0.2–0.5% across all three benchmarks); 'stronger' may require interrogating whether the evidence actually sustains the accuracy-maintenance claim or needs reframing.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty is positioned only against GraphFlow-v1 (same author group); 'stronger' likely demands a clearer delta against the broader heterogeneous-GNN literature, not just the self-comparison.
rationale.R1.6: 'Make stronger' is a canonical prose-improvement directive; sentence-level vagueness ('useful step forward', 'straightforward to implement') weakens rhetorical force and is an obvious target.
